_comment: Canned raw M7 generator proposal for the MDLD602 critical-evaluation subtopic (CLO1-ST2), mirroring Build Spec §2.3. Represents what the node-generation model would emit BEFORE the deterministic projection (no ec ids, no statuses, no frozen FKs). Used by the golden acceptance test so the M7 logic is exercised without a live model.

nodes.knowledge_component: Separate describing a framework from evaluating it with criteria, evidence, and context
nodes.mastery_statement: Given a passage about a curriculum framework, classify whether it describes the framework or critically evaluates it, and justify the classification.
nodes.why_it_matters: Learners who cannot separate description from evaluation produce summaries instead of the criteria-based critique A1 requires.
nodes.node_learning_intent: Describing what a framework says is not the same as judging its quality with criteria, evidence, and context.

solo_descriptors.surface: States that description and evaluation differ.
solo_descriptors.multi_element: Lists features of description and of evaluation separately.
solo_descriptors.relational: Classifies a passage and justifies it by pointing to criteria/evidence/context.
solo_descriptors.extended_abstract: Generalises the distinction to unfamiliar frameworks and mixed passages.

candidate_misconceptions.statement: evaluation means saying whether I like it
candidate_misconceptions.reason: common confusion implied by this distinction node
candidate_misconceptions.suggested_trap: an item that invites an opinion-style answer where a criteria-based judgment is required

nodes.node_title: Identify criteria for evaluating curriculum frameworks
nodes.knowledge_component: Know the criteria a rigorous framework evaluation uses
nodes.mastery_statement: Name and explain the criteria used to evaluate a curriculum framework.
nodes.why_it_matters: Criteria are the backbone of a defensible evaluation.

nodes.node_title: Analyze assumptions behind a curriculum framework
nodes.mastery_statement: Identify and articulate the implicit assumptions a framework rests on.

nodes.node_title: Judge contextual fit of a curriculum framework
nodes.knowledge_component: Decide a framework's suitability for a specific institutional context
nodes.mastery_statement: Given a context, judge whether a framework fits, justifying with criteria and evidence.
nodes.why_it_matters: Fit-for-context is the practical core of curriculum-leadership judgment.

nodes.node_title: Evaluate strengths and limitations using evidence-based criteria
nodes.knowledge_component: Produce a balanced, criteria-based evaluation of a framework
nodes.mastery_statement: Evaluate a framework's strengths and limitations against explicit criteria and evidence.
nodes.why_it_matters: This is the judgment the A1 critical review is graded on.

nodes.node_title: Compare two curriculum frameworks for a specific context
nodes.knowledge_component: Combine criteria-based judgments into a comparative recommendation
nodes.mastery_statement: Compare two frameworks for a given context and recommend one with justification.
nodes.why_it_matters: Integrates the prior nodes into the comparative decision A1 demands.

nodes.node_title: Turn evaluation criteria into design criteria
nodes.knowledge_component: Transfer critique skill toward curriculum design (CLO-4)
nodes.mastery_statement: Reframe evaluation criteria as design criteria for building a framework.

cross_clo_links.reason: Critique criteria become design criteria for framework creation
